Document Title: Data Compliance Policy v1.0
Section 1: Transaction Monitoring
All financial transactions exceeding ten thousand dollars shall be flagged for mandatory compliance
review. Transactions above fifty thousand dollars must be reported to the regulatory authority within
24 hours. Any transaction below one hundred dollars is exempt from monitoring requirements.
Section 2: Customer Identification
Every customer record must contain a valid and non-empty customer identifier. Customer age must
be greater than or equal to 18 years to be eligible for account creation. Users with a credit score
below 300 shall be blocked from loan applications.
Section 3: Data Retention
All transaction records must be retained for a minimum of 7 years. Records older than 10 years shall
be permanently deleted from the system. Backup copies must be created every 30 days.
Section 4: Access Control
Employees must not access customer data without prior authorization. Users with role equal to
admin are required to enable two-factor authentication. Unauthorized access attempts must be
flagged and reported immediately.
Section 5: Risk Classification
Accounts with a risk score greater than 80 shall be escalated for enhanced due diligence.
Transactions from sanctioned regions must be blocked immediately. Customers with more than 5
failed login attempts shall be temporarily suspended.
